top_categories.workout_context: 497 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic SBS Bank Tour of Southland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
497 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what SBS Bank Tour of Southland's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 497 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
SBS Bank Tour of Southland's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 497 miles.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Tempo is SBS Bank Tour of Southland's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 497 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
SBS Bank Tour of Southland's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At SBS Bank Tour of Southland, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of SBS Bank Tour of Southland. Train it until it feels boring.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 497 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 497 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 497 miles of mixed terrain in Invercargill, Southland, New Zealand.
